Cristiano Ronaldo tells Real Madrid to forget about signing this Manchester United target
Yavar Toghrol
03 Apr 2017

​CRISTIANO RONALDO has told Real Madrid to steer clear of signing AC Milan wonderkid Gianluigi Donnarumma, according to reports.


Donnarumma, 18, has been drumming up interest from a host of top European clubs due his impressive form between the sticks at AC Milan.


Real Madrid are keen to find a replacement for keeper Keylor Navas and view Donnarumma as potential candidate to sign this summer. However, Spanish outlet Don Balon claim Cristiano Ronaldo has urged Real chiefs to forget about signing the Italy international.


Ronaldo is apparently against the move due to Donnarumma's choice of agent. Donnarumma is represented by Mino Raiola, who has a personal feud with Jorge Mendes - Ronaldo's manager and friend.


But Real will look to go all out to sign Manchester United keeper David de Gea anyway with Zinedine Zidane making him his No 1 transfer target this summer.


De Gea came close to leaving United for the Bernabeu in 2015 but a deal fell through due to an administrative error.


But Jose Mourinho is apparently eyeing targets in case the Spain international leaves Old Trafford this summer.


Donnarumma and Inter Milan's Samir Handanovic are two options to replace De Gea.


CRISTIANO RONALDO has told Real Madrid to steer clear of signing AC Milan wonderkid Gianluigi Donnarumma, according to reports.
